design_philosophy.core_loop: Select prehistoric beast god, engage in one-on-one combat with pattern-based special moves, defeat opponent, perform finishing move. Repeat against CPU opponents of increasing difficulty or versus human player.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Health depletion race combined with resource management through consuming worshippers to regain health mid-match.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a supernatural prehistoric creature as a god worshipped by human tribes; brutal dominance through special moves and finishing maneuvers in post-apocalyptic setting.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Inverted control scheme (hold buttons, then perform joystick movement for special moves) deliberately differs from Street Fighter standard; incorporates worship/consumption mechanic as strategic health recovery distinct from traditional fighting game design; humorous finishing moves (e.g., transforming opponent into laughing cow) alongside brutal ones, blending dark tone with absurdist humor.

difficulty.continues_system: Single-player campaign escalates through CPU opponents of increasing difficulty; final battle (difficulty level 10+) is endurance round against all seven monsters sequentially.
difficulty.checkpointing: Campaign structure with progressive opponent encounters; minigame opportunity before final endurance battle to consume worshippers and increase health bar.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player selects difficulty level; higher difficulties unlock final all-monster endurance battle. No explicit difficulty selection within single-player campaign flow documented.

progression.upgrade_system: Health recovery through consuming worshippers during matches (player's worshippers) or opponent's worshippers (score bonus). Minigame prior to final battle allows accumulated health increase for endurance round.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Final battle containing all seven monsters accessible only at difficulty level 10 or higher.
replayability_hooks: Seven distinct playable characters with specialized move sets (Blizzard, Armadon, Talon, Sauron, Chaos, Vertigo, Diablo)
Seven unique background stages corresponding to character domains
Multiple finishing moves per character with varying comedy/brutality balance
Hidden easter egg minigames (human volleyball, bowling)

world_building_techniques: Post-apocalyptic setting (meteor strike, human civilization regression to Stone Age tribes)
Thematic stage environments reflecting character domains (Cliff, Hollows, Strip, Cove, Ruins, Tomb, Inferno)
Character archetypes as gods divided between peace and chaos factions
Worshipper tribes in arena backgrounds demonstrating deity veneration
Continental landmass shaped as fire-breathing dinosaur skull (world structure encodes apocalyptic narrative)

boss_design.philosophy: Final challenge is endurance gauntlet (all seven monsters fought sequentially with increased power bars) accessible only at highest difficulty. Minigame mechanic allows preparation through worshipper consumption. No single dominant boss pattern documented.
notable_bosses: Final endurance battle: all seven fighters with increased power bars in sequence
Diablo (tyrannosaur-like god of evil) - final opponent of apocalyptic gauntlet

faction_indicators: Seven prehistoric gods divided into peace faction (Blizzard, Armadon, Talon) and chaos faction (Sauron, Chaos, Vertigo, Diablo) based on plot
Human tribes segregated and aligned with specific gods they worship
Character alignment tied to divine domain (e.g., Blizzard=good, Diablo=evil, Chaos=decay)
danger_signals: Consumption of human worshippers as resource mechanic (cannibalism/sacrifice theme)
Brutal finishing maneuvers alongside humorous ones (tonal dissonance)
Post-apocalyptic tribalism: human civilization regression after meteor strike
Chaos and decay as divine forces competing with peace and survival
environmental_storytelling: Stage environments encode character identity and domain (Inferno for Diablo, Tomb for Vertigo, Cove for Sauron)
Worshipper presence in arenas demonstrates divine authority and human subjugation
Primordial rainforest setting indicates reclaimed/untamed world post-civilization
Planetary transformation: continental landmass reshaped into dinosaur skull form

sources_used: wikipedia - Primal Rage article (gameplay, plot, development, character roster, finishing moves, special move mechanics, stage design)
